questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Which of the following technological features is correctly matched with its intended purpose in the Census 2027 framework?

options.text: Census-as-a-service (CaaS): A mobile application for citizens to perform self-enumeration.
Census Management & Monitoring System (CMMS): A real-time portal for monitoring the census process.
National Population Register (NPR): A clean, machine-readable data format for inter-ministerial use.
Civil Registration System (CRS): A dedicated portal for real-time monitoring of enumerators.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct as the CMMS portal is developed for real-time monitoring. Option A is incorrect because CaaS is a delivery model for ministries, not a citizen app. Option C is incorrect as CaaS (not NPR) provides clean, machine-readable data to ministries. Option D is incorrect as CRS is for birth/death registration, not census monitoring.
questions.takeaway: CMMS is for process monitoring, while CaaS is for data delivery to government stakeholders.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: New Technology: A dedicated Census Management & Monitoring System (CMMS) portal has been developed for real-time monitoring of the process. Census-as-a-service (CaaS) will deliver data to ministries in a clean, machine-readable and actionable format.
